app.browserOnlyBackend: You’re running the frontend only (npm run dev). Launch Rotate with npm run desktop to use the vault and integrations.

sidebar.footerHint: Everything stays on this device. Lock the session when you are done.

settings.title: Vault preferences
settings.subtitle: Tune how the app behaves on this device.
settings.sessionLead: When the timer expires, Rotate asks for your password before opening the vault again.
settings.sessionHint: The maximum is 8 hours. Saving realigns the current session to the new duration.
settings.passwordLead: Your password protects the local vault. Rotate cannot show it or recover it for you.
settings.passwordHint: Use at least {{min}} characters. Below 16 characters, use at least 3 categories among lowercase, uppercase, numbers, and symbols.
settings.passwordWeak: The new password must have at least {{min}} characters and be less predictable.
settings.passwordMismatch: The two new passwords do not match.
settings.passwordSame: The new password must be different from the current one.

home.emptyDescription: Pick a provider in Explore and connect it to your workspace. You rotate only the assets you choose—nothing else in the account is touched.
home.withServicesSubtitle: Open any account below for keys, rotation, and connection settings.

explore.title: Which providers to manage here
explore.subtitle: Add only what you actually use. Every rotation is scoped: one resource at a time, never a blind reset of the whole account.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare
cloudflare.blurb: Link Account ID and management token: list tokens, rotate with the same policies, secrets stored securely.

vercel.title: Vercel
vercel.blurb: Connect a Vercel token to update project environment variables after a rotation.

supabase.title: Supabase
supabase.blurb: Project keys and tenant secrets. When supported, sensitive actions stay behind the vault.

oauth_google.title: OAuth (Google)
oauth_google.blurb: Client ID and secret for OAuth flows. Usually configured in Google Cloud Console.

services.empty: No services yet. Go to Explore to connect your first account.
services.title: Your accounts
services.subtitle: Click a row to open the account and manage tokens and settings.

placeholder.body: Rotate doesn’t support this provider yet. Full controls—account linking, keys, and rotation—will ship in a later update.

vault.setupBody: This password unlocks the local vault. If you lose it, Rotate cannot open saved integrations or secrets.
vault.pinMin: The password must be at least {{min}} characters.
vault.pinMismatch: The two passwords do not match.

changePin.title: Change vault password
changePin.newMin: The new password must be at least {{min}} characters and less predictable.
changePin.mismatch: The two new password entries do not match.

cloudflare.intro: The management token is stored locally by Rotate. To list and rotate tokens you need at least <0>Account · API Tokens · Read</0> and <1>Edit</1> (create and delete).
cloudflare.connectLead: This key is used only by Rotate to read and create Cloudflare tokens.
cloudflare.reconnectLead: The account is already known, but the internal token required to list and rotate keys is missing.
cloudflare.keyringHint: Rotate saves this credential on this device and prefers the system keyring when available.
cloudflare.rotateByIdLead: If the token does not appear in the list, paste its Cloudflare ID here. Rotate will clone its policies, show the new secret, and can revoke the old token.
cloudflare.noTokens: No tokens returned. Check that the management token has the right permissions.
cloudflare.footnote: Rotation creates a new token with the same policies. The secret is shown only once—copy it now. Rotate's management token stays separate from tracked tokens.
cloudflare.confirmUnlink: Remove the Account ID and token from this device
cloudflare.confirmReveal: Anyone who can see your screen can read the token. Continue
cloudflare.rotateLead: A new token will be created with the same policies as <0>{{name}}</0>.
cloudflare.rotateUpdateVault: Update the token saved in Rotate (required if you rotate the token this app uses for Cloudflare).
cloudflare.rotateRevoke: Revoke the previous token after the new one is created.
cloudflare.rotateWarn: Before revoking the old token, update the destinations where it is used. Management-token rotation will use a separate flow.
cloudflare.resultLead: Store this value now—the Cloudflare dashboard will not show it again.
cloudflare.revealLead: Use in a private setting—don’t share your screen. Close as soon as you’re done.
cloudflare.clipboardHint: Clipboard will clear automatically in about 30 seconds.
